title: Supersonic Review Finding
description: The ONE canonical finding + review-result shape emitted by every Supersonic review skill (layout-review, copy-review, visual-qa, accessibility-review, seo-auditor) and by the machine audits (tools/a11y-audit.mjs). Before this contract each review skill emitted its own finding fields and its own severity scale, so layout-review could not actually merge the other reviewers' output. This schema fixes one severity enum (blocker|major|minor|nit) and one finding shape so findings are machine-mergeable across skills (worst-severity-wins on a shared rule_id+target), and it carries the loop-termination fields (round, escalated_upstream, dismissed) that make the review loop bounded instead of prose. A review skill emits one envelope per reviewed target; each issue is a $defs.finding. The a11y audit emits findings with tool_proof set. This schema documents the contract; tools/validate-agent-quality.mjs (npm run agents:check) checks that each review skill references it and that reports use the canonical Proof Summary.

skill.description: Which review surface produced this result.

target_ref.description: What was reviewed: a page_id, a pattern slug, a QA page URL, or a report path.

band.description: Overall grade for the grading review skills (layout-review, copy-review). Omit for leaf audits that only emit findings.

round.description: Feedback-loop round. The loop is capped at 2 rounds: round 1 scores and emits fixes; round 2 verifies the fixes. A blocker that recurs in round 2 escalates upstream instead of opening a round 3.

escalated_upstream.description: True when a recurring blocker (same rule_id + target after a fix) was routed to the upstream builder (layout-architect / pattern-builder / seo-strategist) instead of re-looping. When true, band must be blocked.

dismissed.description: Findings considered and dismissed; the authoritative do-not-re-flag set the next round must exclude.

severity.description: The one canonical severity scale. blocker = must fix before approval; major = fix before ship; minor = should fix; nit = optional polish. Map axe impact critical->blocker, serious->major, moderate->minor, minor->nit. Map the former visual-qa scale critical->blocker, high->major, medium->minor, low->nit, nit->nit.

target.description: Where the finding lives. At least one of file/slot/selector should be set; the merge key is (rule_id + whichever target identity is present).

file.description: Source file path, e.g. a pattern .php or a data file.

selector.description: DOM selector or pattern slug (visual / a11y).

rule_id.description: Rule namespace id, e.g. COLOR-3, A11Y-1, CONV-1, COMPOSE-4, axe/color-contrast.

dimension.description: Human dimension name, e.g. color-contrast, persuasion, heading-hierarchy.

evidence.description: Concrete proof of the finding: measured value, quote, or screenshot path.

suspected_source.description: The pattern/section/upstream skill likely responsible, for blame-routing.

money_page.description: copy-review: this slot is on a primary conversion page; fixes get the higher-tier model.

tool_proof.description: Machine evidence when the finding came from a tool (axe, lighthouse). Distinguishes measured findings from judgment.
